text: Fill in the blanks on histology and embryology of the abdomen and pelvis
questions: The GIT wall has a universal four-layer structure: mucosa, submucosa, *muscularis externa*, and serosa or adventitia.
*Brunner's/Brunner* glands in the submucosa are the distinguishing histological feature of the duodenum.
*Peyer's/Peyer* patches (aggregated lymphoid follicles) are the distinguishing histological feature of the ileum.
The double-bubble sign on X-ray in a neonate suggests *duodenal* atresia, caused by failure of recanalisation of the duodenum.
The epithelium of the GIT is derived from *endoderm*, while the surrounding connective tissue and muscle are from splanchnic mesoderm.
The liver and pancreas develop as endodermal outgrowths (buds) of the *foregut*.
The kidney develops from *intermediate* mesoderm in three successive waves: pronephros, mesonephros, and metanephros.
Hirschsprung's disease results from failure of migration of *neural crest* cells to the distal bowel, causing aganglionosis.
In the absence of testosterone, the *Mullerian/Müllerian* duct system persists to form the female reproductive tract.
*Parietal/Oxyntic* cells in the fundic glands of the stomach secrete hydrochloric acid and intrinsic factor.
